strategic_note: LibreYOLO is a MIT-code open-source library that provides the same API as Ultralytics but with permissive licenses. It is NOT an external competitor — it is a core VisionServeX-supported ecosystem. libreyolo-* models belong in the core ledger, not external_restricted_baselines.

model_families.notes: RF-DETR Roboflow core detector (NOT the XL/2XL PML variants). Apache-2.0.

v2.48 audit: LibreYOLO distributes YOLOv9 via MultimediaTechLab/YOLO which relicensed under MIT (not the original WongKinYiu GPL-3.0 repo). HF model card LibreYOLO/LibreYOLO9s shows MIT. Updated from GPL-3.0 to MIT in VisionServeX license table.

model_families.exclusion_reason: Deci.AI proprietary non-commercial license. Blocked by default.
model_families.notes: YOLO-NAS uses Deci.AI license; not commercial-safe without agreement.

task_support_confirmed.segmentation: YOLOX, D-FINE, RT-DETR, RF-DETR (confirmed from HF model names ending -seg)
task_support_confirmed.pose: Not confirmed for LibreYOLO distribution (GitHub says YOLOX/YOLOv9/YOLO-NAS but no HF model found)
task_support_confirmed.tracking: ByteTrack integration via libreyolo[tracking] — not a separate model family
task_support_confirmed.export: ONNX, TorchScript, TensorRT, OpenVINO, NCNN via libreyolo[onnx/tensorrt/openvino/ncnn]

yolov9_license_correction: Changed GPL-3.0 → MIT based on HF model card evidence from MultimediaTechLab/YOLO relicensing.
